name: GPT-Realtime-2 + Voice API
summary: three voice models in the API — GPT-Realtime-2 brings GPT-5-class reasoning and 128K context to live speech-to-speech, GPT-Realtime-Translate covers 70 input / 13 output languages, GPT-Realtime-Whisper streams transcription; $32/M audio input, built for production voice agents

name: Gemini 3.1 Flash-Lite
summary: cheapest Gemini 3 model now generally available — $0.25/M input and $1.50/M output, sub-second classification latency, multimodal inputs (text/image/video/audio/PDF); live in AI Studio and Vertex AI

name: GitHub Spec Kit
summary: open-source toolkit for spec-driven development — turns vague prompts into a four-phase workflow (Specify → Plan → Tasks → Implement) that runs against Claude Code, Copilot, Gemini CLI, and 30+ other agents; free, MIT, hit 90K+ stars in days

name: Claude Dreaming + Multi-Agent Orchestration
summary: at Code with Claude, Anthropic shipped 'dreaming' — a scheduled process that mines past agent sessions for patterns and curates them into reusable skills — and moved outcomes and multi-agent orchestration into public beta on Claude Managed Agents

name: GPT-5.5 Instant
summary: ChatGPT's new default model — 52.5% fewer hallucinations, 30% shorter responses, and personalization from past chats, files, and connected Gmail; live for all ChatGPT users and as chat-latest in the API

name: Cloudflare + Stripe Agent Provisioning
summary: open protocol that lets coding agents create Cloudflare accounts, register domains, grab API tokens, and deploy code with no human in the loop — Stripe handles payment with a default $100/month per-provider cap

name: Midjourney V8.1
summary: image gen update — HD mode 3x faster and 3x cheaper than V8.0, standard renders 50% faster and 25% cheaper, brings back image prompts and adds a Prompt Shortener; live on midjourney.com and Discord

name: Cursor SDK
summary: TypeScript SDK that turns Cursor's coding agent into programmable infrastructure — npm install @cursor/sdk, run agents locally or on Cursor's sandboxed cloud VMs from CI, backend services, or your own product, billed per-token
